blocking_findings: Prism M-E2E review reports 1 high + 2 medium findings, ALL false positives on louke/_tests.py:18,37,47 — these are pattern-LITERAL STRINGS in the Prism anti-pattern scanner's own regex pattern definitions (r"assert\s+True\s*==\s*True", r"pytest\.mark\.skip", r"mock\.patch..."), not real anti-patterns. The Prism scanner has no skip-self mechanism so it scans its own source. Master user (zillionare) accepted this risk as a known scanner design issue (same family as the M-DEV eval-exec false-positive on re.compile); not introduced by v0.11-001-web-ide, just surfaced by including louke/_tests.py in the diff (the file was edited to broaden AC ID matching from FR-only to FR|NFR, which is a v0.11 AC-trace requirement). Recommend follow-up issue to add self-exclusion to louke/_tests.py (and louke/_security.py) so scanner pattern literals are not flagged; out of scope for v0.11 web ide.
accepted_risks: Prism test-pattern scanner self-detection: louke/_tests.py:18 (trivial-assert: regex pattern literal r"assert\s+True\s*==\s*True"), louke/_tests.py:37 (skip-without-issue: regex pattern literal r"pytest\.mark\.skip"), louke/_tests.py:47 (mock-overuse: regex pattern literal r"mock\.patch"). All three are literal pattern strings in the scanner's TEST_ANTI_PATTERNS list (i.e. the regex used to DETECT these patterns in user tests), not actual anti-patterns. Recommend follow-up to add self-exclusion: scan_test_file() should skip if 'louke/_tests.py' in str(filepath) and 'louke/_tools/' in str(filepath) (the latter hosts the matching scanner).

metadata.approved_by: zillionare (Master user via Maestro session 2026-07-11 22:14 UTC)
